senses.definition: rare or obsolete term referring to the act or process of providing or constructing a scaffold.
examples: The craftsmen discussed the scaffolage required for the old building.

senses.definition: plural of scaffolage; scarce usage referring to multiple scaffoldings or acts of scaffolding.
examples: Several scaffolages were erected around the site.

senses.definition: a temporary structure used by workers to reach higher parts of a building or other large object.
examples: The workers climbed the scaffold to repair the roof.
They erected a scaffold around the facade.

senses.definition: to provide a scaffold for support or to temporarily support a process or idea.
examples: The teacher scaffolded the lesson for beginners.
The project was scaffolded by an initial pilot study.

senses.definition: the act or process of erecting or using scaffolding; scaffolding material or work.
examples: Scaffoldage was completed before sunrise.

senses.definition: plural form of scaffoldage; multiple instances of scaffolding work or material.
examples: Several scaffoldages lined the street.

senses.definition: provided with a scaffold; supported or arranged in a way that supports learning or development temporarily.
examples: The lesson was scaffolded to help beginners understand the concept.

senses.definition: a person who builds or maintains scaffolding; a scaffold builder.
examples: The scaffolder inspected the planks before use.

senses.definition: plural of scaffolder; workers who construct or maintain scaffolding.
examples: Several scaffolders arrived to start work.

senses.definition: a framework used to support people or objects while working at height.
examples: They set up scaffolding around the building.

senses.definition: a system of support or temporary structure used to help learning or progress step by step.
examples: The teacher provided scaffolding for the new topic.

senses.definition: plural of scaffolding; multiple scaffolding structures or systems.
examples: Various scaffoldings were erected around the site.

senses.definition: plural of scaffold; multiple temporary structures used for support while working at height.
examples: The workers climbed the scaffolds to reach the facade.

senses.definition: to provide or erect scaffolds for support or access.
examples: They scaffold the building with new planks.

senses.definition: slang or informal shortened form related to scaffolding contexts; may appear in dialectal use.
examples: He kicked the scafs aside to pass.

senses.definition: informal or playful term relating to scaffolding; used as slang in some regions.
examples: A scaffy structure blocks the street.

senses.definition: slang for a piece of heroin or a stray piece of filth; also used as a verb meaning to scavenge or trash-fy; regional.
examples: The dealer avoided the area full of scags.

senses.definition: to scag someone; to criticize harshly or to scavenge; dialectal.
examples: They scagged the old walls clean.

senses.definition: past tense of scag; to scuff or harshly criticize; or to discard as scag.
examples: The walls were scagged by the storm.

senses.definition: act of scagging; harsh criticism or scavenging behavior in slang.
examples: The scagging continued for hours.

senses.definition: a term in art/stonework referring to a fragment of shale or fossil fragment; used in mosaics or inlay work.
examples: The floor was inlaid with scaglia pieces.

senses.definition: plural of scaglia; pieces of scaglia used in mosaic or inlay.
examples: They placed several scaglias in the border.

senses.definition: a decorative material, often a type of marble-like plaster used for inlay work in architecture.
examples: The columns were clad in scagliola to imitate marble.

senses.definition: plural of scagliola.
examples: The cabinets were decorated with several scagliolas.

senses.definition: a person who collects or specializes in scagliola pieces.
examples: He is a scagliolist who studies antique inlays.

senses.definition: plural form of scag in slang usage; can refer to pieces of discarded material or drugs depending on dialect.
examples: They dumped the scags by the curb.

senses.definition: a surname or proper name; also may refer to a piece of metal or tool depending on context; primarily a proper noun in usage.
examples: Ms. Scaife published a paper.

senses.definition: a Scottish or archaic term for a shoe or selling area; also variant spellings exist.
examples: He found a scail at the market.

senses.definition: past tense of scail; to shoe or to refer to in dialectal form.
examples: The boat scailed across the lake.
